description: Information sheet This information sheet lists the Cost of Treatment Regulations amounts ACC can pay for medical practitioner, nurse and paramedic treatments. unit $ (excl. GST) Per unit $ (incl. GST) Per hour $ (excl. GST) Per hour $ (incl. GST) Flat rate $ (excl. GST) Flat rate $ (incl. GST) Nurses and nurse practitioners
Nurse consultation if the client is 14 years old or over
Nurse consultation if the client is under 14 years old
Nurse consultation – Community Services Card holder
Nurse consultation – dependant of Community Services Card holder (14 -17 years)
Nurse practitioner consultation if the client is 14 years old or over
Nurse practitioner consultation if the client is under 14 years old
Nurse practitioner consultation – Community Services Card holder
Nurse practitioner consultation – Dependant of Community Services Card holder (14 – 17 years) Combined
Nurse/GP consultation – if the client is 14 years old or over
Nurse/GP consultation – if the client is under 14 years old
Nurse/GP consultation – Community Services Card holder
Nurse/GP consultation – dependant of Community Services Card holder (14 -17 years)
Nurse/Nurse Practitioner consultation – if the client is 14 years old or over
Nurse/Nurse Practitioner consultation – if the client under 14 years old
Nurse/Nurse Practitioner consultation – Community Services Card holder
Nurse/Nurse Practitioner consultation – dependant of Community Services Card holder (14 -17 years) Paramedics
Paramedic consultation if the client is 14 years old or over
Paramedic consultation if the client is under 14 years old
Paramedic consultation – Community Services Card holder
Paramedic consultation – dependant of Community Services Card holder (14 -17 years) Medical practitioners
GP consultation – if the client is 14 years old or over (also known as CON)
GP consultation – if the client is under 14 years old
GP consultation - Community Services Card holder
GP consultation – dependant of Community Services Card holder (14 -17 years)
GP Emergency travel per km
Attendance (including travel time) at an emergency Burn/Abrasion
Treatment of burn less than 4cm²
Treatment of burn at a single site greater than 4cm²
Treatment of significant abrasions less than 4cm² at a single site
Treatment of significant abrasions greater than 4cm² at a single site
Significant burns or abrasions (not including fractures) at multiple sites (greater than 4cm²): necessary wound cleaning, preparation, and dressing Dislocation
Dislocation of finger/toe with splint/strapping
Dislocation of thumb: closed reduction and immobilisation
Dislocation of elbow with radiological confirmation: closed reduction and immobilisation
Dislocation of shoulder: closed reduction and collar and cuff immobilisation
Dislocation of patella: closed reduction and cast immobilisation
Fractured finger or toe (proximal, middle, or distal phalanx): closed reduction and immobilisation
Fractured finger or toe (proximal, middle, or distal phalanx): requiring local anaesthetic
Fractured metatarsal: closed reduction (not requiring cast): immobilisation by strapping
Fractured metacarpal(s) hand: with or without local anaesthetic: immobilisation by strapping
Fractured carpal bone, including scaphoid: treatment by cast immobilisation, not requiring reduction
Fractured tarsal or metatarsal bones (excluding calcaneum or talus): treatment by cast immobilisation
Fractured calcaneum or talus: treatment by cast immobilisation
Fractured clavicle
Fractured distal radius and ulna: cast immobilisation not requiring reduction
Fractured distal radius and ulna requiring closed reduction, involving regional or other form of anaesthesia
Fractured shaft radius and ulna: treatment by cast immobilisation
Fractured distal humerus (supracondylar or condylar): by cast immobilisation
Fractured proximal or shaft humerus: immobilisation by collar and cuff or U-slab
Fractured shaft tibia and/or fibula: treatment by cast immobilisation with reduction
Fractured distal tibia and/or fibula: treatment by cast immobilisation with reduction
Fractured fibula (without tibial fracture): immobilisation with soft tissue strapping Maternal Birth Injuries
Internal Examination for Maternal Birth Injury Miscellaneous
Abscess or haematoma: drainage with incision (with or without local anaesthetic agent)
Insertion of IV line for administration of IV medications or electrolytes or transfusion (if provided under local or national guideline approved by ACC)
Nail, simple removal of
Nail, removal of or wedge resection: requiring the use of digital anaesthesia

Removal of embedded or impacted foreign body from cornea or conjunctiva (with use of topical anaesthetic), or from auditory canal or nasal passages, or from skin or subcutaneous tissue with incision, or from rectum or vagina
Pinch skin graft
Dental anaesthetic
Epistaxis: arrest during episode by nasal cavity packing with or without cautery Open wound
Closure of open wounds less than 2cm: any necessary care and treatment including cleaning and debriding, exploration, administration of anaesthetic, and dressing

Closure of open wound (or wounds) of skin and subcutaneous tissue or mucous membrane 2cm to 7cm long: any necessary care and treatment including cleaning and debriding, exploration, administration of anaesthetic, and dressing

Closure of open wound (or wounds) of skin and subcutaneous tissue or mucous membrane greater than 7cm long: any necessary care and treatment including cleaning and debriding, exploration, administration of anaesthetic, and dressing
Amputation of digit, including use of anaesthetic, debridement of bone and soft tissue, and closure of wound
Simple soft tissue injuries: management of simple sprain of wrist/ankle/knee/elbow or other soft tissue injury requiring crepe bandage or similar immobilisation not requiring formal strapping

Soft tissue injury (other than splinting of dislocated or fractured digit), unless specified elsewhere: application of plaster or padded splint or specific strapping within agreed guidelines (includes splinting of Achilles tendon injury and serious ankle sprains)
Aspiration of inflamed joint, tendon, bursa or other subcutaneous tissue or space (with or without injection)
Extensor tendon, primary repair
Ruptured Tendon Achilles; management by plaster immobilisation Ear Toileting by Ear Nurse Specialists and Nurses not in general practice
Ear/Aural Toileting by microscope – Ear Nurse Specialist
Ear Toileting by general nurse (not in General Practice)
